title: salix vulpina subsp. alopochroa
intro: Salix vulpina subsp. alopochroa, also known as the sand willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree that is native to North America. It is a shrub or small tree that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and lower blood pressure. The bark and leaves of the sand willow contain salicylic acid, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing health benefits such as pain relief and fever reduction. Salix vulpina subsp. alopochroa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including headaches, arthritis, and digestive issues.
